{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SmYDoxZHj4pVVjF1hNMbb4","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f3839fd8-bcf5-4155-bb9e-482a94ac4b2d","uuid":"5d04081e-8235-4a50-a7c8-327677d14e23","timestamp":"2026-03-21T01:00:07.336Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwXMU1vkRDgKVp1K4CXaJG","type":"tool_result","content":"MEMORY.md\npatterns.md\nplatforms.md\n# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint has warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #49: Accessibility improvements — ARIA, keyboard nav, screen readers (merged + deployed)\n- #46: Loading skeletons, error boundaries, improved error handling (merged + deployed)\n- #43: Hardening: rate limiting, navbar perf, error handling, DB indexes (merged)\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check issue #21 for human response on Telegram token\n2. Consider proposing: image optimization with next/image, domain + HTTPS setup\n3. Check production health via /api/health\n4. All approved issues are complete — look for new improvements to propose\n# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n# Registered Platforms\n\n## GitHub\n- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n\n## Production Deployment\n- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint has warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #49: Accessibility improvements — ARIA, keyboard nav, screen readers (merged + deployed)\n- #46: Loading skeletons, error boundaries, improved error handling (merged + deployed)\n- #43: Hardening: rate limiting, navbar perf, error handling, DB indexes (merged)\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check issue #21 for human response on Telegram token\n2. Consider proposing: image optimization with next/image, domain + HTTPS setup\n3. Check production health via /api/health\n4. All approved issues are complete — look for new improvements to propose\n# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Q9UavAbKF2FnczvpxiRTSH","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_016i2jVzU3pF1PFbfRfXaWik","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue list --state all --limit 50 --json number,title,labels,state 2>/dev/null | python3 -c \"\nimport json,sys\nissues = json.load(sys.stdin)\nfor i in issues:\n    labels = ','.join(l['name'] for l in i['labels'])\n    print(f\\\"#{i['number']} [{i['state']}] {labels:30s} {i['title']}\\\")\n\"","description":"List all issues with labels"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4574,"cache_read_input_tokens":13766,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":4574},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f3839fd8-bcf5-4155-bb9e-482a94ac4b2d","uuid":"1d2e8f39-ded5-45c0-bcee-636c87e6f1f8"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011bHMEiSqdmpczsvFH2o9Ch","type":"tool_result","content":"# Decisions Log\n\n## 2026-03-19: Next.js 15 over 16\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root directory)\n- Downgraded to Next.js 15 which uses webpack by default\n- No feature loss for our use case\n\n## 2026-03-19: SQLite for MVP\n- Per PRD, using SQLite with file storage in app/data/\n- Simple, no external dependencies\n- Good enough for MVP; can migrate to Postgres later if needed\n\n## 2026-03-19: iron-session for auth\n- Lightweight session management with encrypted httpOnly cookies\n- No separate session store needed\n- Password hashing with bcrypt (12 rounds)\n\n## 2026-03-19: Session logs gitignored\n- Session logs contain raw API data that may include tokens\n- Added to .gitignore to prevent secret leaks via GitHub push protection","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f3839fd8-bcf5-4155-bb9e-482a94ac4b2d","uuid":"ff8f56c9-238e-469a-adbc-b196f90f1e31","timestamp":"2026-03-21T01:00:29.295Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"# Decisions Log\n\n## 2026-03-19: Next.js 15 over 16\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root directory)\n- Downgraded to Next.js 15 which uses webpack by default\n- No feature loss for our use case\n\n## 2026-03-19: SQLite for MVP\n- Per PRD, using SQLite with file storage in app/data/\n- Simple, no external dependencies\n- Good enough for MVP; can migrate to Postgres later if needed\n\n## 2026-03-19: iron-session for auth\n- Lightweight session management with encrypted httpOnly cookies\n- No separate session store needed\n- Password hashing with bcrypt (12 rounds)\n\n## 2026-03-19: Session logs gitignored\n- Session logs contain raw API data that may include tokens\n- Added to .gitignore to prevent secret leaks via GitHub push protection","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KC8D9WNtjfLYvTXN5vjbTD","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→\n     5→interface BookResult {\n     6→  key: string;\n     7→  title: string;\n     8→  author_name?: string[];\n     9→  cover_i?: number;\n    10→}\n    11→\n    12→interface Props {\n    13→  onSelect: (book: {\n    14→    title: string;\n    15→    author: string;\n    16→    coverUrl: string;\n    17→    olid: string;\n    18→  }) => void;\n    19→}\n    20→\n    21→export default function BookSearch({ onSelect }: Props) {\n    22→  const [query, setQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    23→  const [results, setResults] = useState<BookResult[]>([]);\n    24→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    25→  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);\n    26→  const [activeIndex, setActiveIndex] = useState(-1);\n    27→  const timeoutRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    28→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    29→  const listboxRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    30→\n    31→  useEffect(() => {\n    32→    const handler = (e: MouseEvent) => {\n    33→      if (\n    34→        containerRef.current &&\n    35→        !containerRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)\n    36→      ) {\n    37→        setOpen(false);\n    38→      }\n    39→    };\n    40→    document.addEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    41→    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    42→  }, []);\n    43→\n    44→  useEffect(() => {\n    45→    if (query.length < 3) {\n    46→      setResults([]);\n    47→      return;\n    48→    }\n    49→\n    50→    if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    51→\n    52→    timeoutRef.current = setTimeout(async () => {\n    53→      setLoading(true);\n    54→      try {\n    55→        const res = await fetch(\n    56→          `https://openlibrary.org/search.json?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&limit=8&fields=key,title,author_name,cover_i`\n    57→        );\n    58→        const data = await res.json();\n    59→        setResults(data.docs || []);\n    60→        setOpen(true);\n    61→        setActiveIndex(-1);\n    62→      } catch {\n    63→        setResults([]);\n    64→      } finally {\n    65→        setLoading(false);\n    66→      }\n    67→    }, 400);\n    68→\n    69→    return () => {\n    70→      if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    71→    };\n    72→  }, [query]);\n    73→\n    74→  const handleSelect = (book: BookResult) => {\n    75→    const olid = book.key.replace(\"/works/\", \"\");\n    76→    onSelect({\n    77→      title: book.title,\n    78→      author: book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\",\n    79→      coverUrl: book.cover_i\n    80→        ? `https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-M.jpg`\n    81→        : \"\",\n    82→      olid,\n    83→    });\n    84→    setQuery(book.title);\n    85→    setOpen(false);\n    86→    setActiveIndex(-1);\n    87→  };\n    88→\n    89→  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {\n    90→    if (!open || results.length === 0) return;\n    91→\n    92→    switch (e.key) {\n    93→      case \"ArrowDown\":\n    94→        e.preventDefault();\n    95→        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev < results.length - 1 ? prev + 1 : 0));\n    96→        break;\n    97→      case \"ArrowUp\":\n    98→        e.preventDefault();\n    99→        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev > 0 ? prev - 1 : results.length - 1));\n   100→        break;\n   101→      case \"Enter\":\n   102→        e.preventDefault();\n   103→        if (activeIndex >= 0 && activeIndex < results.length) {\n   104→          handleSelect(results[activeIndex]);\n   105→        }\n   106→        break;\n   107→      case \"Escape\":\n   108→        setOpen(false);\n   109→        setActiveIndex(-1);\n   110→        break;\n   111→    }\n   112→  };\n   113→\n   114→  // Scroll active option into view\n   115→  useEffect(() => {\n   116→    if (activeIndex >= 0 && listboxRef.current) {\n   117→      const activeEl = listboxRef.current.children[activeIndex] as HTMLElement;\n   118→      if (activeEl) {\n   119→        activeEl.scrollIntoView({ block: \"nearest\" });\n   120→      }\n   121→    }\n   122→  }, [activeIndex]);\n   123→\n   124→  const activeDescendant =\n   125→    activeIndex >= 0 && results[activeIndex]\n   126→      ? `book-option-${results[activeIndex].key}`\n   127→      : undefined;\n   128→\n   129→  return (\n   130→    <div ref={containerRef} className=\"relative\">\n   131→      <input\n   132→        type=\"text\"\n   133→        className=\"input-field\"\n   134→        value={query}\n   135→        onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}\n   136→        onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}\n   137→        placeholder=\"Search for a book title...\"\n   138→        onFocus={() => results.length > 0 && setOpen(true)}\n   139→        role=\"combobox\"\n   140→        aria-expanded={open && results.length > 0}\n   141→        aria-haspopup=\"listbox\"\n   142→        aria-controls=\"book-search-listbox\"\n   143→        aria-activedescendant={activeDescendant}\n   144→        aria-label=\"Search for a book\"\n   145→        aria-autocomplete=\"list\"\n   146→      />\n   147→      {loading && (\n   148→        <div\n   149→          className=\"absolute {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Qgd8JvmxHjHqeFrZydfPLf","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→\n    76→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    77→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    78→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    83→    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    84→    decided_at TEXT,\n    85→    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    86→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    87→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    88→  );\n    89→`);\n    90→\n    91→// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval column\n    92→try {\n    93→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n    94→} catch {\n    95→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→// Idempotent migration: add platform_preference column\n    99→try {\n   100→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram'\");\n   101→} catch {\n   102→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→// Idempotent migration: add discord_link column\n   106→try {\n   107→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_link TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   108→} catch {\n   109→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   110→}\n   111→\n   112→// Idempotent migration: add discord_channel_id column\n   113→try {\n   114→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   115→} catch {\n   116→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→// Idempotent migration: add telegram_chat_id column for auto-telegram feature\n   120→try {\n   121→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN telegram_chat_id INTEGER\");\n   122→} catch {\n   123→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   124→}\n   125→\n   126→// Tables for Telegram bot integration\n   127→db.exec(`\n   128→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n   129→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   130→    telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   131→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   132→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id),\n   133→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n   134→  );\n   135→\n   136→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n   137→    chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n   138→    chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   139→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n   140→  );\n   141→\n   142→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_user_links (\n   143→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   144→    telegram_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   145→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   146→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id),\n   147→    UNIQUE (telegram_user_id),\n   148→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   149→  );\n   150→`);\n   151→\n   152→// Rating and Reputation system\n   153→db.exec(`\n   154→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n   155→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n   156→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   157→    rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   158→    rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   159→    score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n   160→    comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   161→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   162→    UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n   163→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n   164→    FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n   165→    FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   166→  );\n   167→`);\n   168→\n   169→// Performance indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\n   170→db.exec(`\n   171→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\n   172→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\n   173→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\n   174→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n   175→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_applications_listing_status ON listing_applications(listing_id, status);\n   176→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_unread ON notifications(user_id, is_read);\n   177→`);\n   178→\n   179→export default db;\n   180→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UjNqkzWp5DmW8hjhcDCEfy","session_id":"f3839fd8-bcf5-4155-bb9e-482a94ac4b2d","uuid":"d7199397-f34d-4f4e-a3a3-afd708b533a3","timestamp":"2026-03-21T01:01:25.876Z"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017Du3fsjjHP3q5CuFbGaoqi","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→\n     6→interface Listing {\n     7→  id: number;\n     8→  book_title: string;\n     9→  book_author: string;\n    10→  book_cover_url: string;\n    11→  language: string;\n    12→  reading_pace: string;\n    13→  start_date: string;\n    14→  meeting_format: string;\n    15→  max_group_size: number;\n    16→  requires_approval: number;\n    17→  platform_preference: string;\n    18→  member_count: number;\n    19→  author_name: string;\n    20→  created_at: string;\n    21→}\n    22→\n    23→export default function HomePage() {\n    24→  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n    25→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    26→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n    27→\n    28→  // Search & filter state\n    29→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    30→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n    31→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    32→  const [startDateFrom, setStartDateFrom] = useState(\"\");\n    33→  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n    34→  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n    35→\n    36→  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    37→\n    38→  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    39→    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string, pace?: string, dateFrom?: string) => {\n    40→      setLoading(true);\n    41→      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    42→      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n    43→      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n    44→      if (pace) params.set(\"reading_pace\", pace);\n    45→      if (dateFrom) params.set(\"start_date_from\", dateFrom);\n    46→      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n    47→\n    48→      const qs = params.toString();\n    49→      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`)\n    50→        .then((r) => {\n    51→          if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load listings\");\n    52→          return r.json();\n    53→        })\n    54→        .then((data) => {\n    55→          setListings(data.listings || []);\n    56→          setError(null);\n    57→          setLoading(false);\n    58→        })\n    59→        .catch((err) => {\n    60→          setError(err.message || \"Could not load listings. Please try again.\");\n    61→          setLoading(false);\n    62→        });\n    63→    },\n    64→    []\n    65→  );\n    66→\n    67→  // Initial fetch\n    68→  useEffect(() => {\n    69→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\");\n    70→  }, [fetchListings]);\n    71→\n    72→  // Debounced search\n    73→  const handleSearchChange = (value: string) => {\n    74→    setSearchQuery(value);\n    75→    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    76→    debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n    77→      fetchListings(value, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom);\n    78→    }, 400);\n    79→  };\n    80→\n    81→  // Immediate filter changes\n    82→  const handleFormatChange = (value: string) => {\n    83→    setMeetingFormat(value);\n    84→    fetchListings(searchQuery, value, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom);\n    85→  };\n    86→\n    87→  const handlePaceChange = (value: string) => {\n    88→    setReadingPace(value);\n    89→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, value, startDateFrom);\n    90→  };\n    91→\n    92→  const handleStartDateFromChange = (value: string) => {\n    93→    setStartDateFrom(value);\n    94→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, value);\n    95→  };\n    96→\n    97→  const handleSortChange = (value: string) => {\n    98→    setSort(value);\n    99→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, value, readingPace, startDateFrom);\n   100→  };\n   101→\n   102→  const clearFilters = () => {\n   103→    setSearchQuery(\"\");\n   104→    setMeetingFormat(\"\");\n   105→    setReadingPace(\"\");\n   106→    setStartDateFrom(\"\");\n   107→    setSort(\"newest\");\n   108→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\");\n   109→  };\n   110→\n   111→  const hasActiveFilters = searchQuery || meetingFormat || readingPace || startDateFrom || sort !== \"newest\";\n   112→\n   113→  const formatMeetingType = (f: string) => {\n   114→    const labels: Record<string, string> = {\n   115→      voice: \"Voice calls\",\n   116→      text: \"Text chat\",\n   117→      mixed: \"Mixed\",\n   118→    };\n   119→    return labels[f] || f;\n   120→  };\n   121→\n   122→  return (\n   123→    <div>\n   124→      <div className=\"text-center mb-6 sm:mb-8 mt-2 sm:mt-4\">\n   125→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-4xl mb-2 sm:mb-3\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\" }}>\n   126→          Find your reading companion\n   127→        </h1>\n   128→        <p\n   129→          className=\"text-sm sm:text-lg max-w-xl mx-auto\"\n   130→          style={{\n   131→            color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   132→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   133→          }}\n   134→        >\n   135→          Browse open reading groups, join one that matches your pace, and\n   136→          connect on Telegram to read together.\n   137→        </p>\n   138→      </div>\n   139→\n   140→      {/* Search & Filter Bar */}\n   141→      <div className=\"mb-6\">\n   142→        <div className=\"flex gap-3 items-center\">\n   143→          <div className=\"flex-1 relative\">\n   144→            <input\n   145→              type=\"text\"\n   146→              placeholder=\"Search by book title or author...\"\n   147→              value={searchQuery}\n   148→              onChange={(e) => handleSearchChange(e.target.value)}\n   149→              className=\"input-field\"\n   150→              style={{ paddingLeft: \"2.5rem\" }}\n   151→            />\n   152→            <svg\n   153→              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   154→              style={{\n   155→                position: \"absolute\",\n   156→                left: \"0.75rem\",\n   157→                top: \"50%\",\n   158→                transform: \"translateY(-50%)\",\n   159→                color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   160→                width: \"1.125rem\",\n   161→                height: \"1.125rem\",\n   162→                pointerEvents: \"none\",\n   163→              }}\n   164→              fill=\"none\"\n   165→              stroke=\"currentColor\"\n   166→              strokeWidth={2}\n   167→              viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n   168→            >\n   169→              <circle cx={11} cy={11} r={8} />\n   170→              <line x1={21} y1={21} x2={16.65} y2={16.65} />\n   171→            </svg>\n   172→          </div>\n   173→          <button\n   174→            onClick={() => setFiltersOpen(!filtersOpen)}\n   175→            className=\"btn-secondary\"\n   176→            aria-expanded={filtersOpen}\n   177→            aria-controls=\"filter-panel\"\n   178→            style={{\n   179→              display: \"flex\",\n   180→              alignItems: \"center\",\n   181→              gap: \"0.375rem\",\n   182→              whiteSpace: \"nowrap\",\n   183→              position: \"relative\",\n viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n   191→              fill=\"none\"\n   192→              stroke=\"currentColor\"\n   193→              strokeWidth={2}\n   194→            >\n   195→              <line x1={4} lg:grid-cols-4 gap-3 sm:gap-4 items-end\"\n   226→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   227→            >\n   228→              <div>\n   229→                <label\n className=\"input-field\"\n   267→                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n   268→                >\n   269→                  <option value=\"\">All paces</option>\n   270→                  <option value=\"chapter\">Chapter-based</option>\n   271→                  <option value=\"pages\">Pages-based</option>\n   272→                  <option >\n   286→                  Starting from\n   287→                </label>\n   288→                <input\n   289→                  type=\"date\"\n   290→                  value={startDateFrom}\n (\n   344→        <div className=\"grid gap-4\">\n   345→          {[1, 2, 3].map((i) => (\n   346→            <div key={i} className=\"card flex gap-4\">\n   347→              <div\n   348→                className=\"skeleton\"\n   349→                style={{ width: \"4rem\", height: \"6rem\", flexShrink: 0 }}\n   350→              />\n   351→              <div className=\"flex-1\">\n   352→                <div\n   353→                  className=\"skeleton skeleton-heading\"\n   354→                  style={{ width: `${60 - i * 5}%` }}\n   355→                />\n   356→                <div\n   357→                  className=\"skeleton skeleton-text\"\n   358→                  style={{ width: `${45 - i * 5}%` }}\n   359→                />\n   360→                <div className=\"flex gap-2 mt-2\">\n   361→                  <div\n   362→                    className=\"skeleton\"\n   363→                    style={{\n   364→                      width: \"5rem\",\n   365→                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n   366→                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n   367→                    }}\n   368→                  />\n   369→                  <div\n   370→                    className=\"skeleton\"\n   371→                    style={{\n   372→                      width: \"4rem\",\n   373→                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n   374→                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n   375→                    }}\n   376→                  />\n   377→                  <div\n   378→                    className=\"skeleton\"\n   379→                    style={{\n   380→                      width: \"5.5rem\",\n   381→                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n   382→                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n   383→                    }}\n   384→                  />\n   385→                </div>\n   386→              </div>\n   387→            </div>\n   388→          ))}\n   389→        </div>\n   390→      ) : error ? (\n   391→        <div className=\"text-center py-16\">\n   392→          <svg\n   393→            width=\"40\"\n   394→            height=\"40\"\n   395→            viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n   396→            fill=\"none\"\n   397→            stroke=\"var(--color-error)\"\n   398→            strokeWidth={1.5}\n   399→            style={{ margin: \"0 auto 0.75rem\" }}\n   400→          >\n   401→            <circle cx={12} cy={12} r={10} />\n   402→            <line x1={12} y1={8} x2={12} y2={12} />\n   403→            <line x1={12} y1={16} x2={12.01} y2={16} />\n   404→          </svg>\n   405→          <p\n   406→            className=\"text-lg mb-2\"\n   407→            style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   408→          >\n   409→            {error}\n   410→          </p>\n   411→          <button\n   412→            onClick={() => fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom)}\n   413→            className=\"btn-primary\"\n   414→            style={{ marginTop: \"0.75rem\" }}\n   415→          >\n   416→            Try again\n   417→          </button>\n   418→        </div>\n   419→      ) : listings.length === 0 ? const router = useRouter();\n     9→  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n    10→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    11→\n    12→  const [bookTitle, setBookTitle] = setBookCoverUrl(book.coverUrl);\n    33→    setBookOlid(book.olid);\n    34→  };\n    35→\n    36→  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    37→    e.preventDefault();\n    38→    setError(\"\");\n    39→    setLoading(true);\n    40→\n    41→    try {\n    42→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/listings/create\", {\n    43→        method: \"POST\",\n    44→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    45→        body: JSON.stringify({\n    46→          bookTitle,\n    47→          bookAuthor,\n    48→          bookCoverUrl,\n    49→          bookOlid,\n    50→          language,\n    51→          readingPace,\n    52→          startDate,\n    53→          meetingFormat,\n    54→          maxGroupSize,\n    55→          requiresApproval,\n    56→          platformPreference,\n    57→        }),\n    58→      });\n    59→\n    60→      const data = await res.json();\n    61→\n    62→      if (!res.ok) {\n    63→        if (res.status === 401) {\n    64→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    65→          return;\n    66→        }\n    67→        setError(data.error || \"Failed to create listing\");\n    68→        return;\n    69→      }\n    70→\n    71→      router.push(`/listings/${data.id}`);\n    72→    } catch {\n    73→      setError(\"Something went wrong. Please try again.\");\n    74→    } finally {\n    75→      setLoading(false);\n    76→    }\n    77→  };\n    78→\n    79→  return (\n    80→    <div className=\"max-w-lg mx-auto\">\n    81→      <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-3xl mb-2\">Start a reading group</h1>\n    82→      <p\n    83→        className=\"mb-6 sm:mb-8\"\n    84→        style={{\n    85→          color: style={{\n    97→            backgroundColor: \"rgba(192, 57, 43, 0.08)\",\n    98→            color: \"var(--color-error)\",\n    99→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   100→          }}\n   101→          role=\"alert\"\n   102→        >\n   103→          {error}\n   104→        </div>\n   105→      )}\n   106→\n   107→      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className=\"space-y-5\">\n   108→        <div>\n   109→          <label\n   110→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   111→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, 51→export default function ListingDetail() {\n    52→  const { id } = useParams();\n    53→  const router = useRouter();\n    54→  const [listing, setListing] = useState<Listing | null>(null);\n are not critical\n   128→    }\n   129→  }, [id]);\n   130→\n   131→  useEffect(() => {\n   132→    if (listing?.is_full && listing?.isMember) {\n   133→      fetchRatings();\n   134→    }\n   135→ useCallback } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     6→\n     7→interface ReadingItem {\n     8→  id: number;\n     9→  book_title: string;\n    10→  book_author: string;\n    11→  book_cover_url: string;\n    12→  reading_pace: string;\n    13→  start_date: string;\n    14→  meeting_format: string;\n    15→  is_full: number;\n    16→  telegram_link: string;\n    17→  member_count: number;\n    18→  max_group_size: number;\n    19→  author_name: string;\n    20→  joined_at: string;\n    21→}\n    22→\n    23→interface Reputation {\n    24→  averageScore: number;\n    25→  totalRatings: number;\n    26→  completedGroups: number;\n    27→  groupsRated: number;\n    28→  breakdown: Record<number, number>;\n    29→  recentRatings: {\n    30→    score: number;\n    31→    comment: string;\n    32→    created_at: string;\n    33→    book_title: string;\n    34→  }[];\n    35→}\n    36→\n    37→type TabKey = \"profile\" | \"reading\" | \"genres\" | \"reputation\";\n    38→\n    39→export default function ProfilePage() {\n    40→  const router = useRouter();\n    41→  const [displayName, setDisplayName] = useState(\"\");\n    42→  const [bio, setBio] = useState(\"\");\n    43→  const [email, setEmail] = useState(\"\");\n    44→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    45→  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);\n    46→  const [message, setMessage] = useState(\"\");\n    47→\n    48→  // Tab state\n    49→  const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<TabKey>(\"profile\");\n    50→\n    51→  // Reading activity\n    52→  const [active, setActive] = useState<ReadingItem[]>([]);\n    53→  const [history, setHistory] = useState<ReadingItem[]>([]);\n    54→  const [upcoming, setUpcoming] = useState<ReadingItem[]>([]);\n    55→  const [readingLoading, setReadingLoading] = useState(false);\n    56→\n    57→  // Genres\n    58→  const [userGenres, setUserGenres] = useState<string[]>([]);\n    59→  const [availableGenres, setAvailableGenres] = useState<string[]>([]);\n    60→  const [genresLoading, setGenresLoading] = useState(false);\n    61→  const [genresSaving, setGenresSaving] = useState(false);\n    62→  const [genresMessage, setGenresMessage] = useState(\"\");\n    63→\n    64→  // Reputation\n    65→  const [reputation, setReputation] = useState<Reputation | null>(null);\n    66→  const [reputationLoading, setReputationLoading] = useState(false);\n    67→\n    68→  useEffect(() => {\n    69→    fetch(\"/api/auth/me\")\n    70→      .then((r) => r.json())\n    71→      .then((data) => {\n    72→        if (!data.user) {\n    73→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    74→          return;\n    75→        }\n    76→        setDisplayName(data.user.displayName);\n    77→        setBio(data.user.bio || \"\");\n    78→        setEmail(data.user.email);\n    79→        setLoading(false);\n    80→      })\n    81→      .catch(() => {\n    82→        setLoading(false);\n    83→      });\n    84→  }, [router]);\n    85→\n    86→  const fetchReading = useCallback(() => {\n    87→    setReadingLoading(true);\n    88→    fetch(\"/api/profile/reading\")\n    89→      .then((r) => r.json())\n    90→      .then((data) => {\n    91→        setActive(data.active || []);\n    92→        setHistory(data.history || []);\n    93→        setUpcoming(data.upcoming || []);\n    94→        setReadingLoading(false);\n    95→      })\n    96→      .catch(() => setReadingLoading(false));\n    97→  }, []);\n    98→\n    99→  const fetchGenres = useCallback(() => {\n   100→    setGenresLoading(true);\n   101→    fetch(\"/api/profile/genres\")\n   102→      .then((r) => r.json())\n   103→      .then((data) => {\n   104→        setUserGenres(data.genres || []);\n   105→        setAvailableGenres(data.available || []);\n   106→        setGenresLoading(false);\n   107→      })\n   108→      .catch(() => setGenresLoading(false));\n   109→  }, []);\n   110→\n   111→  const fetchReputation = useCallback(() => {\n   112→    setReputationLoading(true);\n   113→    fetch(\"/api/profile/reputation\")\n   114→      .then((r) => r.json())\n   115→      .then((data) => {\n   116→        setReputation(data.reputation || null);\n   117→        setReputationLoading(false);\n   118→      })\n   119→      .catch(() => setReputationLoading(false));\n   120→  }, []);\n   121→\n   122→  useEffect(() => {\n   123→    if (activeTab === \"reading\") fetchReading();\n   124→    if (activeTab === \"genres\") fetchGenres();\n   125→    if (activeTab === \"reputation\") fetchReputation();\n   126→  }, [activeTab, fetchReading, fetchGenres, fetchReputation]);\n   127→\n   128→  const handleSave = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n   129→    e.preventDefault();\n   130→    setSaving(true);\n   131→    setMessage(\"\");\n   132→\n   133→    try {\n   134→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/profile\", {\n   135→        method: \"PATCH\",\n   136→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n   137→        body: JSON.stringify({ displayName, bio }),\n   138→      });\n   139→      const data = await res.json();\n   140→      if (res.ok) {\n   141→        setMessage(\"Profile updated!\");\n   142→      } else {\n   143→        setMessage(data.error || \"Failed to update\");\n   144→      }\n   145→    } catch {\n   146→      setMessage(\"Something went wrong\");\n   147→    } finally {\n   148→      setSaving(false);\n   149→    }\n   150→  };"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UjNqkzWp5DmW8hjhcDCEfy","session_id":"f3839fd8-bcf5-4155-bb9e-482a94ac4b2d","uuid":"3aa6906d-7634-4f1e-8174-4ba3f830024d","timestamp":"2026-03-21T01:01:30.800Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01AgadCr8t7zaAD3ikbfEZPm","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→\n     4→export interface SessionData {\n     5→  userId?: number;\n     6→  email?: string;\n     7→  displayName?: string;\n     8→}\n     9→\n    10→const DEV_SECRET =\n    11→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    12→\n    13→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    14→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    15→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    16→    throw new Error(\n    17→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→  return {\n    21→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    22→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    23→    cookieOptions: {\n    24→      httpOnly: true,\n    25→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\n    26→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    27→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    28→    },\n    29→  };\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→export async function getSession() {\n    33→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    34→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    35→}\n    36→\n    37→export async function requireAuth() {\n    38→  const session = await getSession();\n    39→  if (!session.userId) {\n    40→    return null;\n    41→  }\n    42→  return session;\n    43→}\n    44→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UjNqkzWp5DmW8hjhcDCEfy","session_id":"f3839fd8-bcf5-4155-bb9e-482a94ac4b2d","uuid":"7de9610f-d494-4ae7-a2ba-50fa060a9162","timestamp":"2026-03-21T01:01:37.382Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016A8r8WTV3xzuCiW2J4p1qz","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→import { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n     3→\n     4→function getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n     5→  const user = db\n     6→    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n     7→    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n     8→  return user?.email || null;\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→export function createNotification(\n    12→  userId: number,\n    13→  listingId: number,\n    14→  type: string,\n    15→  message: string\n    16→) {\n    17→  db.prepare(\n    18→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    19→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    20→\n    21→  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n    22→  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n    23→  if (email) {\n    24→    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    25→    sendEmail(email, subject, message).catch(() => {\n    26→      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    27→    });\n    28→  }\n    29→}\n    30→\n    31→function getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n    32→  switch (type) {\n    33→    case \"new_member\":\n    34→      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    35→    case \"group_full\":\n    36→      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    37→    case \"application_received\":\n    38→      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    39→    case \"application_approved\":\n    40→      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    41→    case \"application_rejected\":\n    42→      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    43→    case \"rating_received\":\n    44→      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    45→    default:\n    46→      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    47→  }\n    48→}\n    49→\n    50→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    51→  listingId: number,\n    52→  joinerName: string\n    53→) {\n    54→  const listing = db\n    55→    .prepare(\n    56→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    57→    )\n    58→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    59→\n    60→  if (listing) {\n    61→    createNotification(\n    62→      listing.author_id,\n    63→      listingId,\n    64→      \"new_member\",\n    65→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    66→    );\n    67→  }\n    68→}\n    69→\n    70→export function notifyApplicationReceived(\n    71→  listingId: number,\n    72→  applicantName: string\n    73→) {\n    74→  const listing = db\n    75→    .prepare(\n    76→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    77→    )\n    78→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    79→\n    80→  if (listing) {\n    81→    createNotification(\n    82→      listing.author_id,\n    83→      listingId,\n    84→      \"application_received\",\n    85→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    86→    );\n    87→  }\n    88→}\n    89→\n    90→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n    91→  userId: number,\n    92→  listingId: number,\n    93→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n    94→) {\n    95→  const listing = db\n    96→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    97→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n    98→\n    99→  if (listing) {\n   100→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   101→    const message =\n   102→      decision === \"approved\"\n   103→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   104→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   105→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   106→  }\n   107→}\n   108→\n   109→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   110→  const listing = db\n   111→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   112→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   113→  const members = db\n   114→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   115→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   116→\n   117→  if (listing) {\n   118→    const botConfigured = !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   119→    const message = botConfigured\n   120→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The Telegram group will be set up automatically.`\n   121→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`;\n   122→\n   123→    for (const member of members) {\n   124→      createNotification(\n   125→        member.user_id,\n   126→        listingId,\n   127→        \"group_full\",\n   128→        message\n   129→      );\n   130→    }\n   131→  }\n   132→}\n   133→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UjNqkzWp5DmW8hjhcDCEfy","session_id":"f3839fd8-bcf5-4155-bb9e-482a94ac4b2d","uuid":"57d80cee-7c7a-4aa1-84a7-3e415192b654","timestamp":"2026-03-21T01:01:37.618Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01N25yEcNM1iJoK8oYGVJCaB","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Telegram Bot API integration for automatic group creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate deep links for one-click group creation\n     6→ * 2. Detect when the bot is added to a group (via webhook)\n     7→ * 3. Auto-generate and save invite links\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Create Telegram Group\" → opens deep link in Telegram\n    11→ * - Telegram prompts author to create a group with the bot\n    12→ * - Bot receives `my_chat_member` update via webhook\n    13→ * - Bot creates an invite link and saves it to the listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const BOT_TOKEN = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    17→const TELEGRAM_API = `https://api.telegram.org/bot${BOT_TOKEN}`;\n    18→\n    19→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    20→  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0;\n    21→}\n    22→\n    23→/**\n    24→ * Call the Telegram Bot API.\n    25→ */\n    26→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    27→  method: string,\n    28→  params?: Record<string, unknown>\n    29→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; result?: T; description?: string }> {\n    30→  const res = await fetch(`${TELEGRAM_API}/${method}`, {\n    31→    method: \"POST\",\n    32→    headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    33→    body: JSON.stringify(params || {}),\n    34→  });\n    35→  return res.json();\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→/**\n    39→ * Get bot info (username, id).\n    40→ */\n    41→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    42→  id: number;\n    43→  username: string;\n    44→  first_name: string;\n    45→} | null> {\n    46→  const res = await callApi<{\n    47→    id: number;\n    48→    username: string;\n    49→    first_name: string;\n    50→  }>(\"getMe\");\n    51→  return res.ok ? res.result! : null;\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→/**\n    55→ * Generate a deep link that opens Telegram and prompts the user\n    56→ * to create a new group with the bot already added.\n    57→ *\n    58→ * Format: https://t.me/BOT_USERNAME?startgroup=listing_LISTING_ID\n    59→ *\n    60→ * When the user creates the group, Telegram adds the bot and\n    61→ * sends a `my_chat_member` update to our webhook.\n    62→ */\n    63→export async function generateGroupDeepLink(\n    64→  listingId: number\n    65→): Promise<string | null> {\n    66→  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n    67→  if (!bot) return null;\n    68→  return `https://t.me/${bot.username}?startgroup=listing_${listingId}`;\n    69→}\n    70→\n    71→/**\n    72→ * Create an invite link for a chat where the bot is an admin.\n    73→ */\n    74→export async function createChatInviteLink(\n    75→  chatId: number | string\n    76→): Promise<string | null> {\n    77→  const res = await callApi<{ invite_link: string }>(\n    78→    \"createChatInviteLink\",\n    79→    {\n    80→      chat_id: chatId,\n    81→      name: \"Bookmate Reading Group\",\n    82→      creates_join_request: false,\n    83→    }\n    84→  );\n    85→  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n    86→    return res.result.invite_link;\n    87→  }\n    88→  return null;\n    89→}\n    90→\n    91→/**\n    92→ * Export the existing invite link for a chat (fallback).\n    93→ */\n    94→export async function exportChatInviteLink(\n    95→  chatId: number | string\n    96→): Promise<string | null> {\n    97→  const res = await callApi<string>(\"exportChatInviteLink\", {\n    98→    chat_id: chatId,\n    99→  });\n   100→  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n   101→    return res.result;\n   102→  }\n   103→  return null;\n   104→}\n   105→\n   106→/**\n   107→ * Send a message to a Telegram chat.\n   108→ */\n   109→export async function sendMessage(\n   110→  chatId: number | string,\n   111→  text: string\n   112→): Promise<boolean> {\n   113→  const res = await callApi(\"sendMessage\", {\n   114→    chat_id: chatId,\n   115→    text,\n   116→    parse_mode: \"HTML\",\n   117→  });\n   118→  return res.ok;\n   119→}\n   120→\n   121→/**\n   122→ * Set up webhook URL for receiving Telegram updates.\n   123→ */\n   124→export async function setWebhook(url: string): Promise<boolean> {\n   125→  const res = await callApi(\"setWebhook\", {\n   126→    url,\n   127→    allowed_updates: [\"my_chat_member\", \"message\"],\n   128→  });\n   129→  return res.ok;\n   130→}\n   131→\n   132→/**\n   133→ * Parse the listing ID from a /start command payload.\n   134→ * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n   135→ */\n   136→export function parseListingIdFromPayload(\n   137→  payload: string\n   138→): number | null {\n   139→  const match = payload.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n   140→  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n   141→}\n   142→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UjNqkzWp5DmW8hjhcDCEfy","session_id":"f3839fd8-bcf5-4155-bb9e-482a94ac4b2d","uuid":"67380f6f-5bb3-4479-b463-e820860e0732","timestamp":"2026-03-21T01:01:37.962Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Rz1t8SpEdgY35kEAQTQrjA","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Simple in-memory rate limiter for auth endpoints.\n     3→ * Uses a sliding window approach keyed by identifier (IP + route).\n     4→ * Not shared across cluster workers — acceptable for SQLite single-process setup.\n     5→ */\n     6→\n     7→interface RateLimitEntry {\n     8→  timestamps: number[];\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→const store = new Map<string, RateLimitEntry>();\n    12→\n    13→// Clean up expired entries every 5 minutes\n    14→const CLEANUP_INTERVAL = 5 * 60 * 1000;\n    15→setInterval(() => {\n    16→  const now = Date.now();\n    17→  for (const [key, entry] of store) {\n    18→    entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < 15 * 60 * 1000);\n    19→    if (entry.timestamps.length === 0) {\n    20→      store.delete(key);\n    21→    }\n    22→  }\n    23→}, CLEANUP_INTERVAL);\n    24→\n    25→/**\n    26→ * Check if a request should be rate limited.\n    27→ * @param key - Unique identifier (e.g., IP + email for login, IP for register)\n    28→ * @param maxAttempts - Maximum attempts allowed in the window\n    29→ * @param windowMs - Time window in milliseconds (default: 15 minutes)\n    30→ * @returns Object with allowed boolean and retryAfter seconds (0 if allowed)\n    31→ */\n    32→export function checkRateLimit(\n    33→  key: string,\n    34→  maxAttempts: number = 5,\n    35→  windowMs: number = 15 * 60 * 1000\n    36→): { allowed: boolean; retryAfter: number } {\n    37→  const now = Date.now();\n    38→  let entry = store.get(key);\n    39→\n    40→  if (!entry) {\n    41→    entry = { timestamps: [] };\n    42→    store.set(key, entry);\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Remove expired timestamps\n    46→  entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < windowMs);\n    47→\n    48→  if (entry.timestamps.length >= maxAttempts) {\n    49→    const oldestInWindow = entry.timestamps[0];\n    50→    const retryAfter = Math.ceil((oldestInWindow + windowMs - now) / 1000);\n    51→    return { allowed: false, retryAfter };\n    52→  }\n    53→\n    54→  entry.timestamps.push(now);\n    55→  return { allowed: true, retryAfter: 0 };\n    56→}\n    57→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UjNqkzWp5DmW8hjhcDCEfy","session_id":"f3839fd8-bcf5-4155-bb9e-482a94ac4b2d","uuid":"22b24a0a-4c9a-497a-84ea-a5406880a9b3","timestamp":"2026-03-21T01:01:37.993Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DM5n5jCrdE6N7SydFoVE6K","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import nodemailer from \"nodemailer\";\n     2→\n     3→const isConfigured =\n     4→  !!process.env.SMTP_HOST &&\n     5→  !!process.env.SMTP_PORT &&\n     6→  !!process.env.SMTP_FROM;\n     7→\n     8→let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n     9→\n    10→function getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n    11→  if (!isConfigured) return null;\n    12→  if (!transporter) {\n    13→    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n    14→      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n    15→      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n    16→      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n    17→      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n    18→        ? {\n    19→            auth: {\n    20→              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n    21→              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n    22→            },\n    23→          }\n    24→        : {}),\n    25→    });\n    26→  }\n    27→  return transporter;\n    28→}\n    29→\n    30→export async function sendEmail(\n    31→  to: string,\n    32→  subject: string,\n    33→  text: string,\n    34→  html?: string\n    35→): Promise<boolean> {\n    36→  const t = getTransporter();\n    37→  if (!t) return false;\n    38→\n    39→  try {\n    40→    await t.sendMail({\n    41→      from: process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n    42→      to,\n    43→      subject,\n    44→      text,\n    45→      html: html || text,\n    46→    });\n    47→    return true;\n    48→  } catch (err) {\n    49→    console.error(\"Failed to send email:\", err);\n    50→    return false;\n    51→  }\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→export function isEmailConfigured(): boolean {\n    55→  return isConfigured;\n    56→}\n    57→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UjNqkzWp5DmW8hjhcDCEfy","session_id":"f3839fd8-bcf5-4155-bb9e-482a94ac4b2d","uuid":"eec3628d-0e47-48dc-a3c9-4df03edbe795","timestamp":"2026-03-21T01:01:39.908Z"}
